trimsSummary: Sold in the US only as the Q4 plug-in hybrid. Sprint, Ti, and Veloce trims. Turbo 1.3L I4 (FWD) plus a 121-hp rear electric motor = eAWD, 285 hp / 347 lb-ft combined. 15.5-kWh battery, 33 mi EPA electric range, 77 MPGe / 29 mpg combined. AC (Level 1/2) charging only, no DC fast charge. MSRP ~$44,995 (Sprint, incl. $1,595 destination) to ~$49,090 (Veloce).
motorLayout: Turbo 1.3L I4 driving front axle + rear electric motor (through-the-road eAWD)

trimsSummary: First all-electric Jeep. Launch Edition (600 hp / 617 lb-ft, 0-60 3.4 s, ~$70,200) and Limited (500 hp / 524 lb-ft, 0-60 3.9 s, $65,200). 100.5 kWh battery, EPA 294 mi (262 mi with optional 20-in Pirelli tires; Jeep initially targeted ~300 mi). Dual-motor AWD (STLA Large), 400V DC fast up to ~205 kW (5-80% ~28 min). CCS1 port; gains Tesla Supercharger access via NACS adapter in early 2026. No 2026 model year — Jeep paused the Wagoneer S after 2025; a NACS-equipped version is slated to return for 2027.

trimsSummary: Range-extended EV (EREV) full-size pickup, the flagship of Stellantis's PHEV-to-EREV pivot. Two electric motors (250 kW front + 238 kW rear) always drive the truck; a 91.8 kWh battery (69.7 kWh usable) provides EPA 145 mi of electric range, after which a 3.6L Pentastar V6 runs a 130 kW onboard generator for up to 690 mi total range (~20 mpg on gas, 60 MPGe). 663 hp / 615 lb-ft, 0-60 ~4.4 s, 14,000 lb towing, 27-gal tank, 7.2 kW V2H/V2X output. DC fast ~145 kW (20-80% ~45 min). Launching for 2026; trims Tradesman/Big Horn/Laramie/Limited/Tungsten, pricing preliminary (starts slightly above $65,000).
